courses.title: Introduccion a la Escritura Creativa/Introduction to Creative Writing

notes.content: All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.
notes.contentHtml:
All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website.


The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

notes.content: This is a non-screening major: completion of specific major coursework is not required for admission. However, students are strongly encouraged to complete as many lower-division courses as possible from the " Recommended Preparation " section below to support timely degree completion. Students applying to non-screening majors are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements prior to transfer. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor.
notes.contentHtml:
This is a non-screening major: completion of specific major coursework is not required for admission. However, students are strongly encouraged to complete as many lower-division courses as possible from the "Recommended Preparation" section below to support timely degree completion.


Students applying to non-screening majors are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements prior to transfer. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor.

notes.content: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website .
notes.contentHtml: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website.

notes.content: The Literature (LIT) Department at UC Santa Cruz welcomes transfer students. Prospective students interested in majoring in literature are strongly encouraged to complete two lower-division courses, or their articulated equivalents, prior to transferring: LIT 1 – Literary Interpretation One additional lower-division literature course Refer to the "Recommended Preparation" section below for lists of articulated courses approved to fulfill these major requirements. Language Proficiency Requirement All literature majors must demonstrate reading proficiency in a non-English language. Prospective transfer students are strongly recommended to complete at least the first year of non-English language training before transferring, which will prepare them for the advanced coursework required in this major. Students will have the opportunity to consult with a Literature major advisor regarding their prior language coursework and discuss options for satisfying the reading proficiency requirement before enrolling in their first quarter. Creative Writing Concentration Application Acceptance into the UCSC creative writing concentration is by application only. Students interested in this concentration are required to take at least one of the following lower-division creative writing workshops at UC Santa Cruz before submitting their application: LIT 90 – Introduction to Creative Writing LIT 90X – Introducción a la Escritura Creativa/Introduction to Creative Writing LIT 91A – Intermediate Fiction/Prose Writing LIT 91B – Intermediate Poetry Writing To prepare for this concentration, students are strongly encouraged to complete one creative writing course at their community college before transferring, if available. Refer to the “Additional Major Preparation Courses” section below for courses that are comparable to LIT 90, LIT 90X, LIT 91A, or LIT 91B. Additional preparatory creative writing courses that are not included on this list may be offered at your community college; consult with your community college counselor. Please visit the Literature Department website to learn more about this major. UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Literature B.A. Program Statement
notes.contentHtml:
The Literature (LIT) Department at UC Santa Cruz welcomes transfer students. Prospective students interested in majoring in literature are strongly encouraged to complete two lower-division courses, or their articulated equivalents, prior to transferring:


  1. LIT 1 – Literary Interpretation
  2. One additional lower-division literature course


Refer to the "Recommended Preparation" section below for lists of articulated courses approved to fulfill these major requirements.



Language Proficiency Requirement

All literature majors must demonstrate reading proficiency in a non-English language. Prospective transfer students are strongly recommended to complete at least the first year of non-English language training before transferring, which will prepare them for the advanced coursework required in this major. Students will have the opportunity to consult with a Literature major advisor regarding their prior language coursework and discuss options for satisfying the reading proficiency requirement before enrolling in their first quarter.



Creative Writing Concentration Application

Acceptance into the UCSC creative writing concentration is by application only. Students interested in this concentration are required to take at least one of the following lower-division creative writing workshops at UC Santa Cruz before submitting their application:

  • LIT 90 – Introduction to Creative Writing
  • LIT 90X – Introducción a la Escritura Creativa/Introduction to Creative Writing
  • LIT 91A – Intermediate Fiction/Prose Writing
  • LIT 91B – Intermediate Poetry Writing


To prepare for this concentration, students are strongly encouraged to complete one creative writing course at their community college before transferring, if available. Refer to the “Additional Major Preparation Courses” section below for courses that are comparable to LIT 90, LIT 90X, LIT 91A, or LIT 91B. Additional preparatory creative writing courses that are not included on this list may be offered at your community college; consult with your community college counselor.



Please visit the Literature Department website to learn more about this major.

UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Literature B.A. Program Statement
